title: betula × heptopotamica
intro: Betula × heptopotamica, a hybrid of the silver birch (Betula pendula) and the river birch (Betula nana), is a plant species known for its unique characteristics and medicinal properties. The bark of Betula × heptopotamica contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its antiseptic and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Betula × heptopotamica, such as teas and tinctures, can be made by infusing the bark in hot water or a solvent, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds.
